summary: BuildPro full-stack tested. Backend pytest: 14/15 passed (1 sequencing-only failure - test_login_existing ran before register fixture). All real endpoints work: auth (register/login/me + 401/400 negatives), projects CRUD, tasks, quotes (subtotal/tax/total verified - 250/25/275), workers, attendance upsert, inventory, photos, dashboard metrics. AI endpoints exist and return 500 'AI service error' as expected (LiteLLM Budget exceeded - billing issue, NOT a code bug). Frontend: auth screen renders with testIDs (input-email, input-password, auth-submit, tab-login, tab-register), login flow works, navigates to dashboard with metrics, all 4 tabs (HOME/PROJECTS/BILLING/CREW/AI) render correctly with empty states, AI screen has estimate-btn/chat-input/send-btn/suggestion testIDs, ESTIMATE modal opens with all fields (what/area/location/quality econ-std-prem + GENERATE button).

minor.issue: test_login_existing fails when run before the register fixture - cosmetic test issue, backend login itself works (verified via me/duplicate/wrong-password tests). Not a backend bug.
Root path returns 404 (only /api/ is mounted) - low priority.
Returns 500 'AI service error' due to Emergent LLM key Budget exceeded (litellm.BadRequestError). Code is correct per playbook; this is a billing/key budget issue, not a code defect.

integration_issues.issue: Could not visually confirm 'AI ERROR' toast in chat after tapping send-btn (test environment click did not register on TouchableOpacity send icon; element exists with correct testID). API-level 500 confirmed; UI error path needs manual visual verification.

issues: Layout, spacing, contrast, and touch targets look clean and mobile-appropriate.
Bottom tab bar with HOME/PROJECTS/BILLING/CREW/AI is properly sized; metric cards well laid out; primary orange + black theme is consistent.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' - cosmetic deprecation warning from a dependency or component; does not break functionality.

action_items: (Optional) Fix test ordering by renaming test_login_existing -> place after fixture/auth tests or depend on auth fixture; cosmetic only.

Manually verify AI chat UI shows a visible 'AI ERROR' message/toast when /api/ai/chat returns 500 (API confirmed returning 500 with 'AI service error' detail). If not handled, add catch->Alert/inline error in assistant.tsx.
(Low) Address React Native 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' warning by migrating to style.pointerEvents in the relevant component.
(Low) /app/memory/test_credentials.md mentions demo@buildpro.com/demo1234 but is NOT pre-seeded. Either seed it on backend startup or update credentials doc to say 'register first'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses logger inside ai_chat/ai_estimate before logger is defined at module level (defined at line 499, used at 433/477). Works because functions execute after module load, but logically fragile - move logging.basicConfig + logger = ... to top.
JWT tokens have 30-day expiry with HS256; acceptable for MVP.
All Mongo _id excluded via clean(); good. No ObjectId leaks observed in API responses.
CORS uses allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True - this is technically invalid per CORS spec but typical for dev preview.
Auth uses bcrypt + EmailStr - good. Password complexity not enforced (acceptable for MVP).

success_rate.backend: 93% (14/15, the 1 failure is test sequencing only)
success_rate.frontend: ~95% (all screens render and navigate; AI error toast not visually verified)

seed_data_creation: Registered transient test users via API (test_<uuid>@buildpro.com / test1234, ui2_*/ui3_*). All test resources prefixed TEST_ and deleted in same test. No demo@buildpro.com seeded.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend URL env var is EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (NOT EXPO_BACKEND_URL). Auth screen testIDs: tab-login, tab-register, input-email, input-password, auth-submit. Dashboard: logout-btn, metric-active, metric-workers, metric-quotes, metric-lowstock, action-add-circle/receipt/people/sparkles. Assistant: estimate-btn, chat-scroll, suggestion, chat-input, send-btn. AI endpoints WILL return 500 by design (LLM key budget=0); do NOT flag as code bug.
rca_of_issue: AI 500 root cause = litellm.BadRequestError: 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0, Max budget: 0.0' from Emergent LLM key. Pure billing issue, code is per playbook. Mitigation: top up Emergent LLM key budget.
